body:
Bill -

Now that your migration to ETS is complete, I want to get aligned on what you're carrying and how we handle the EES matters still in flight.

Based on your background, the following will be your core ETS responsibilities going forward:

1. Transwestern Rate Proceedings — you own this as pipeline counsel
2. Marketing Affiliate Compliance — given your EES experience, this travels with you to ETS
3. Any FERC tariff or capacity matters touching Northern Natural Gas or Transwestern

As for the three in-flight EES regulatory matters — those need to stay with EES unless they implicate pipeline affiliate obligations. If any of those three have a marketing affiliate compliance component, coordinate a handoff memo to whoever picks them up on the EES side and copy me. Otherwise, you're clean on those once you flag them.

Let me know if any of the EES matters have a pipeline rate or tariff angle that would pull them into your ETS docket. Happy to sort it out on a call.

df

Drew -

Confirmed on all three. I'm owning Transwestern rate proceedings, marketing affiliate compliance, and the NNG/TW tariff and capacity docket going forward.

On the three in-flight EES matters: I need to pull my files and identify them by name before I can give you a definitive read. I expect at least one of them touches marketing affiliate obligations, given the EES regulatory work I was carrying at close. I'll flag those to you and coordinate the handoff memo to the EES side once I've confirmed. I'll have that for you by end of week.

One item worth flagging now: Mary Kay Miller forwarded a Columbia Gulf order last Friday on negotiated rate transactions. FERC is requiring same-day tariff filings on contract terms and actual rates when they change. That has a direct read-through to the TW and NNG deals Mary Kay mentioned, and I'd recommend we track it closely as a tariff compliance matter in my ETS docket.

On the marketing affiliate proposed rule that came out of the September FERC commission meeting — I'll be tracking that as part of my compliance responsibilities here. If you want to loop in ETS on any EES filings already in process on that, let me know.

Happy to sort the rest on a call whenever works for you.

Bill

Good — the Columbia Gulf order is a solid catch and I agree it belongs in your ETS tariff compliance docket. Let's flag it for the next rate proceeding cycle on both TW and NNG.

On the September marketing affiliate proposed rule: yes, loop ETS in on any EES filings already in process. If there's a pipeline affiliate compliance angle in those filings, I want to see the drafts before they go out.

Call whenever you're ready — sounds like we'll have plenty to sort through once you've pulled the EES files. df
